---
title: "Song, Min-Gyu"
---

```{=html}
<style>
body:has(.home-profile) .quarto-title-banner,
body:has(.home-profile) .quarto-title-block,
body:has(.home-profile) #title-block-header {
  display: none !important;
}
</style>
```

::: {.home-profile}

# Song, Min-Gyu  
송민규

Undergraduate Student, Computer Science, Yonsei University  
AI Systems · Machine Learning Systems · Large-Scale Intelligent Systems

## About

::: {.section-lead}
I am an undergraduate student in Computer Science at Yonsei University.
:::

My interests lie in AI systems, machine learning systems, and the structural behavior of large-scale intelligent systems. I am especially interested in how large AI systems behave when they are deployed as real execution systems involving memory, runtime, hardware, inference, and reliability constraints.

Rather than treating AI models only as algorithms, I try to understand them as systems: systems that store, compress, retrieve, schedule, fail, and scale. My current work and study focus on building the technical foundation needed to analyze these systems from both computational and mathematical perspectives.

## Research Interests

* AI Systems
* Machine Learning Systems
* Large-Model Inference
* Agentic AI Reliability
* Runtime and Memory Efficiency
* Mechanistic Understanding of Deep Learning Systems
* Statistical Physics and Information-Theoretic Perspectives on Complex Systems

## Current Work

::: {.section-lead}
I am currently working on independent research projects around large-scale AI systems, with a focus on memory, inference, reliability, and system-level behavior.
:::

My work is motivated by a simple question:

::: {.highlight-box}
How do large intelligent systems preserve, lose, compress, and reorganize information under real computational constraints?
:::

I am also studying how ideas from machine learning systems, parallel computing, statistical physics, and information theory can be used to reason about the behavior and limitations of modern AI systems.

## Academic Service

* Reviewer, ICML 2026 Workshop on Failure Modes in Agentic AI (FAGEN)

## Currently Studying

* **Programming Massively Parallel Processors** — David B. Kirk, Wen-mei W. Hwu
* **Introduction to Machine Learning Systems**
* **Deep Learning** — Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, Aaron Courville
* **vLLM Open Source Runtime**
* **Lectures on Phase Transitions and the Renormalization Group** — Nigel Goldenfeld
* **Information, Physics, and Computation** — Marc Mézard, Andrea Montanari
* **The Road to Reality** — Roger Penrose

## Keywords

AI Systems · ML Systems · Inference Runtime · Memory Efficiency · Agentic AI · Mechanistic Interpretability · Statistical Physics · Information Theory · Complex Systems

:::
